Home › Leptogenesis

Leptogenesis

Leptogenesis is a cosmological mechanism that explains the Matter-Antimatter Asymmetry of the universe as a consequence of an initial asymmetry in the lepton sector. Proposed in 1986 by Fukugita and Yanagida, it suggests that matter exists because of the CP-violating decays of extremely heavy Right-Handed Neutrinos. In university-level astrophysics, leptogenesis favored over other models because it elegantly links the matter puzzle to the observed tiny masses of neutrinos through the Seesaw Mechanism.

1. Heavy Majorana Neutrinos

Standard Model neutrinos are only left-handed and almost massless. Leptogenesis introduces new, heavy right-handed neutrinos (\( N_i \)) with masses near the GUT Scale (\( \sim 10^{10} - 10^{15} ext{ GeV} \)). These particles are Majorana Fermions, meaning they are their own antiparticles. Because they are so heavy, they were only present in the extremely hot Early Universe, where their dynamical behavior dictated the future chemical composition of the cosmos.

2. CP-Violating Decays

As the universe cooled below the mass of these heavy neutrinos, they began to decay into Higgs bosons and Standard Model leptons. If the PMNS Matrix or the heavy neutrino couplings contain a CP-Violating Phase, the rate of decay into leptons (\( N o \ell + H \)) will slightly differ from the rate of decay into antileptons (\( N o \bar{\ell} + H^\dagger \)). This creates a net Lepton Number (\( L \)) in the universe.

3. Sphaleron Conversion (L to B)

The generated lepton asymmetry is not the end of the story. In the high-temperature plasma of the early universe, Sphalerons are active. These non-perturbative fluctuations violate both baryon (\( B \)) and lepton (\( L \)) number but conserve the difference \( B - L \). The sphalerons "process" the lepton surplus, converting roughly 1/3 of it into a Baryon Number surplus. This creates the matter-dominated universe we see today from an initial "seed" in the neutrino sector.

4. Out-of-Equilibrium Decay

For leptogenesis to work, the heavy neutrinos must decay when they are Departure from Thermal Equilibrium. This occurs when the Expansion Rate of the universe is faster than the neutrino decay rate. If they decayed in equilibrium, the inverse reactions would instantly destroy any asymmetry. The successful generation of matter thus requires a specific "timing" between the subatomic decay constant and the Hubble Parameter of the Big Bang.

5. Thermal vs. Resonant Leptogenesis

Physicists distinguish between two types of leptogenesis:

  • Thermal Leptogenesis: Requires extremely high temperatures (\( \gt 10^9 ext{ GeV} \)).
  • Resonant Leptogenesis: Occurs at lower energies (\( \sim 1 ext{ TeV} \)) if two heavy neutrinos have nearly identical masses, amplifying the CP violation via Nuclear Resonance effects.
Resonant models are particularly exciting because they might be reachable by future generations of particle colliders.

6. Experimental Proofs: Neutrino Physics

Since leptogenesis occurs at energy scales far beyond any collider, we must look for its "Shadows" today. The primary tests include:

  • Neutrinoless Double Beta Decay: To prove neutrinos are Majorana particles.
  • CP Violation in Neutrino Oscillations: Measured by the DUNE experiment to verify the CP phase in the PMNS matrix.
  • Light Neutrino Masses: Confirming the Seesaw mechanism that underpins the whole theory.

Key Theoretical Identities

Interact with identities to explore their semantic depth.

  • Initial Asymmetry [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ \epsilon = \frac{\Gamma(N o \ell H) - \Gamma(N o \bar{\ell} H^\dagger)}{\Gamma(N o \ell H) + \Gamma(N o \bar{\ell} H^\dagger)} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Initial Asymmetry: The surplus of neutrinos over antineutrinos that serves as the foundation for matter.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Sector Link [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ n_B = \frac{24 + 4n_H}{66 + 13n_H} n_{B-L} \approx 0.35 \, n_L \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Sector Link: The process that converts lepton number into baryon number in the high-heat Big Bang.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Mass Link [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ M_N \sim 10^{10} - 10^{15} ext{ GeV} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Mass Link: Why the tiny mass of modern neutrinos implies a high-energy origin for the universe's matter.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Efficiency Factor [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ \Delta L eq 0, \Delta B eq 0, \Delta(B-L) = 0 \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Efficiency Factor: The measure of how much CP violation is produced per heavy neutrino decay.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.
